number.title: The Seed of Authority
number.content: The number One represents the singularity of creation, the initial spark from which all subsequent patterns emerge. It is the seed containing the entire holographic blueprint of the trecena, vibrating with unity and focused potential. On this day, the energy is not about scattering your attention but about identifying the core essence of what you wish to cultivate. It brings a feeling of fresh resolve and the power to initiate new cycles. Like a heavy stone dropped into a still pond, the intentions set under this influence ripple outward to define the next thirteen days. It is a time for firmness, clarity, and the solitary strength required to stand upright before the world.

nawal.title: The Pillar of Community
nawal.content: Aj is the energy of the Cane or Reed—the emblem of home, stability, and righteous authority. It represents the spinal column of the human body and the pillars that hold up the roof of the temple. Aj is the gentle but unyielding leader who serves the community not through force, but through integrity and flexible strength. Like the bamboo that sways in the wind but never breaks, Aj teaches the wisdom of resilience. It governs the sanctity of the home space and the protection of those we love. This nawal connects deeply to the Earth and the sky, acting as a conduit between the two, reminding us that true authority comes from being hollow enough to let spirit pass through.

combined_energy.title: Planting the Staff of Power
combined_energy.content: When the initiating force of the number One meets the stabilizing energy of Aj, we encounter a profound moment of grounding. This day is about planting your staff in the earth and claiming your space—not just physical territory, but your spiritual sovereignty. It is the perfect energy for establishing a new order in your life, whether that means revitalizing your home environment, stepping into a leadership role, or simply committing to a personal code of ethics. The atmosphere feels dense with purpose yet supportive, like the strong walls of a sanctuary. You are being asked to define the foundations upon which you will build your future. This is not a day for flighty ideas, but for concrete, tangible commitments that will shelter you and your community for the long haul.
notes: Clarify your non-negotiable values and boundaries today.
Physically clean or bless your home to anchor fresh energy.
Initiate a project that requires long-term stability and patience.
Stand tall in a situation where you have previously made yourself small.
Connect with the earth to stabilize your nervous system and intent.

birthday.title: The Guardian of the Hearth
birthday.content:
To be born on 1 Aj is to be the seed of stability and the anchor for those around you. You carry a natural, quiet authority that does not need to shout to be heard; your presence alone commands respect. You are the architect of safe spaces, instinctively knowing how to build structures—whether they are families, organizations, or physical homes—that endure the tests of time. Your soul's purpose is intertwined with the concept of the 'good leader,' one who leads by example and serves as a pillar of integrity for the community.

You likely possess a deep love for family and tradition, finding strength in your roots. However, your challenge lies in the rigidity that can sometimes accompany such stability. Because you are the 'One,' the initiator, you may feel you must carry the weight of the world alone. Your growth comes from learning the lesson of the reed: to remain upright by being flexible, bending with the winds of change rather than breaking under the pressure of your own high standards.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Aj (Cane/Home) glowing with internal corn-yellow and earthy green light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 1: a single glowing golden dot. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Mirror of Duality
number.content: The number Two introduces the essential tension of polarity: the separation of the one into two, creating the dynamic of relationship. It is the energy of the mirror, reflecting the self back through the other. This vibration governs choices, partnerships, and the delicate balance between opposing forces—light and dark, masculine and feminine, inner and outer. It is not necessarily a day of conflict, but rather one of recognition, where we define ourselves by what we are not. The energy here is reactive and cooperative, asking us to consider the other side of the coin before proceeding.

nawal.title: The Jaguar of Earth Magic
nawal.content: Ix is the jaguar, the shaman of the night, and the keeper of earth magic. It represents the feminine mysteries, intuition, and the high magic of the shrines and altars. Ix is the energy that walks between worlds, seeing in the dark what others miss in the light. It governs our connection to nature spirits and the tangible, vibrating life force of the planet. This nawal is stealthy, powerful, and deeply creative, often associated with the ability to manifest reality through spiritual will. It asks us to honor the invisible forces that shape our physical existence and to treat the earth itself as a living, breathing partner.

combined_energy.title: Harmonizing the Polarities of Nature
combined_energy.content: 2 Ix brings the mystical power of the Jaguar into the realm of relationship and duality. This is a day where the magic of connection is heightened, but so is the potential for projection. The combined energy asks you to look at your partnerships—romantic, business, or even your relationship with nature—and see the magic mirrored there. It is a time to balance your intuitive, wild self with your practical, social obligations. The atmosphere is charged with a stealthy potency; things left unsaid may communicate louder than words. You might feel a pull between the desire for solitude and the need for connection, and the lesson is to find the sacred ground where both can coexist.
notes: Pay attention to signs and synchronicities in your interactions today.
Seek balance between your logical mind and your intuitive gut feelings.
Spend time in nature with a partner or friend to ground the connection.
Notice where you are projecting your own shadow onto others.
Perform a small ritual of gratitude for the relationships that sustain you.

birthday.title: The Shaman of Relationships
birthday.content:
To be born on 2 Ix is to walk the path of the mystical diplomat. You possess a natural affinity for the unseen world and a profound sensitivity to the energies between people. The number Two gifts you with a deep desire for connection and partnership, while the Jaguar grants you the intuition to see behind people's masks. You are likely drawn to healing arts, psychology, or spiritual practices that involve balancing energies. Your presence is magnetic and slightly mysterious, often drawing others to reveal their secrets to you.

However, the duality of the Two can sometimes lead to indecision or a tendency to lose yourself in others. Your challenge is to maintain your own strong, magical center while navigating the complexities of relationships. You are learning to use your powerful intuition not just to understand others, but to create harmony between the spiritual and physical worlds. Your life is a dance of integrating the wild, instinctual wisdom of the Jaguar with the tender, reflective nature of the partner.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Ix (Jaguar/Spots) glowing with internal jungle-green and amber light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 2: two glowing golden dots arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Rhythm of Creation
number.content: The number Three breaks the stalemate of duality and introduces movement, rhythm, and flow. It is the vibration of the triangle, the tripod that creates stability through dynamism. Three represents the integration of the inner and outer to create a third state—expression. It is often associated with creativity, communication, and the restless energy of germination. While it brings the joy of movement, it can also bring instability if not directed, like a stream overflowing its banks. It is a day to let things flow, to experiment, and to trust the rhythm of the process.

nawal.title: The Visionary Bird
nawal.content: Tz'ikin is the eagle, the condor, the bird of power that flies highest and sees furthest. It represents vision, prosperity, and the ability to see the 'big picture' of life. Tz'ikin is the messenger between heaven and earth, bringing news from the spirit world to the physical plane. It governs abundance, not just in money but in relationships, opportunities, and spiritual richness. This nawal encourages us to rise above the minutiae of daily struggles and view our lives from a higher perspective. It is an energy of freedom, lightness, and the joy of seeing dreams manifest into reality.

combined_energy.title: The Flight of Creative Vision
combined_energy.content: 3 Tz'ikin is a day of dynamic, soaring energy. The movement of the number Three combines with the visionary power of the Eagle to create a potent force for manifesting dreams. It is a day where ideas can take flight rapidly, and communication flows with inspiration. You may feel a restless urge to break free from routine and explore new horizons. The energy supports brainstorming, artistic expression, and sharing your vision with others. However, be mindful of scattering your energy too thin; the Eagle sees everything, but must dive with precision to catch its prize. Use the rhythm of the day to propel your long-term goals forward.
notes: Write down your dreams or visions immediately; insights come fast today.
Engage in creative activities that allow your mind to wander freely.
Look for financial or professional opportunities that align with your higher purpose.
Communicate your big-picture ideas to a team or partner.
Take a literal or metaphorical 'view from above' to solve a problem.

birthday.title: The Messenger of Abundance
birthday.content:
To be born on 3 Tz'ikin is to be a restless visionary, forever seeking the horizon. You carry the gift of the Eagle's sight combined with the communicative flair of the number Three. You are likely a natural storyteller, an artist, or an entrepreneur who spots trends long before others do. Your energy is infectious, often lifting the spirits of those around you. You are designed to circulate prosperity and ideas, acting as a bridge between the abstract world of potential and the concrete world of results.

Your challenge lies in the intensity of your mental movement. You may struggle with focus, flitting from one shiny opportunity to the next without landing. Because you see so much, you can become overwhelmed by the possibilities. Your soul's growth comes from learning to harness your powerful rhythm, grounding your vision so that you can manifest the abundance you are destined to create. You are here to show others that true freedom comes from disciplined flight.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Tz'ikin (Bird/Eagle) glowing with internal sky-blue and gold light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 3: three glowing golden dots arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Cube of Stability
number.content: The number Four brings the restless energy of Three into form. It represents the four directions, the four elements, and the solid foundation of the physical world. Four is the energy of stability, definition, and boundaries. It is the cube, the square, the table leg that holds the weight. On this day, the focus shifts to establishing order, defining parameters, and creating a safe container for growth. It is a grounding vibration that asks for practicality and endurance. While it may feel less dynamic, it provides the essential structure needed for any lasting creation.

nawal.title: The Owl of Forgiveness
nawal.content: Ajmaq is the owl, the nawal of deep introspection, forgiveness, and the wisdom of the ancestors. It represents the veil between the living and the dead, and the curiosity to peer behind it. Ajmaq governs the 'sweetness' of life that comes from making peace with our imperfections. It is the energy of pardon—pardoning ourselves for our mistakes and pardoning others. This nawal acknowledges the human condition with all its flaws and teaches that wisdom comes from experience, often through trial and error. It is a gentle, dark, and profound energy that heals the lineage.

combined_energy.title: Grounding the Lineage
combined_energy.content: 4 Ajmaq offers a profound opportunity to stabilize your spiritual and emotional foundations through forgiveness. The structural energy of Four combines with the ancestral depth of Ajmaq to help you build a solid base of inner peace. This is a day to 'settle' debts—energetic, emotional, or karmic. The stability of the Four allows you to look at past mistakes or family patterns without being toppled by them. It is a time to establish a firm boundary against guilt and shame, replacing them with the structural integrity of wisdom learned. You are building a house of forgiveness that will shelter your future growth.
notes: Create a physical altar or space to honor your ancestors.
Write a letter of forgiveness to yourself or someone else (burn it or keep it).
Examine family patterns that need to be stopped or structured differently.
Focus on the practical aspects of a mistake: what was learned, what changes now?
Ground your emotions by physically connecting with the four directions.

birthday.title: The Anchor of Wisdom
birthday.content:
To be born on 4 Ajmaq is to be a stabilizing force for your lineage. You carry the weight of ancestral wisdom with the practical strength to apply it in the modern world. You are likely a 'pattern breaker'—someone who sees the recurring themes in your family history and has the fortitude to resolve them. The number Four gives you a love for structure and safety, while the Owl gives you the curiosity to explore the shadowy, hidden realms of the human psyche. People likely come to you for advice because you offer judgment-free, grounded perspectives.

Your challenge is a tendency to carry too much responsibility or to become rigid in your definitions of right and wrong. You may struggle with perfectionism, forgetting that Ajmaq is about the wisdom of the mistake. Your soul's path is to build a life structure that is flexible enough to include forgiveness—for others, but most importantly, for yourself. You are here to demonstrate that true stability comes from making peace with the past.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Ajmaq (Owl/Lineage) glowing with internal smoky gray and violet light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 4: four glowing golden dots arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Hand of Action
number.content: The number Five represents the center point of the first wave of creation—the four directions plus the center. It is the energy of work, effort, and the human hand. Five breaks the static stability of Four and puts it into motion. It is the number of 'doing,' of gathering resources, and applying will to matter. This vibration carries a sense of urgency and agency; it is not enough to plan, one must now act. It creates a bridge between the intention and the result through the sweat of one's brow. Five empowers us to take the tools we have and put them to use.

nawal.title: The Cosmic Brain
nawal.content: No'j is the nawal of wisdom, intellect, and the sacred science of the mind. It represents the transformation of knowledge into wisdom. No'j is the energy of the earthquake—the sudden shift in the earth that releases pressure and realigns the landscape, just as a new idea shifts our perception. It governs clarity, logic, planning, and the power of thought. This nawal connects us to the universal mind, allowing us to download solutions and innovations. It asks us to use our intelligence not for manipulation, but for the collective good, turning raw data into meaningful understanding.

combined_energy.title: Implementing the Master Plan
combined_energy.content: 5 No'j is a day of powerful, intelligent action. The working energy of Five combines with the intellectual brilliance of No'j to create a vibe of 'practical genius.' This is not a day for abstract theorizing, but for applying your best ideas to solve real-world problems. The energy supports tackling complex tasks, organizing systems, and doing the heavy lifting required to manifest a vision. It is a 'work smart AND hard' kind of day. You may find that solutions to lingering problems suddenly click into place, provided you are willing to put in the effort to implement them. The earthquake energy of No'j suggests that your actions today could significantly shift your landscape.
notes: Tackle the most complex problem on your to-do list today.
Organize your workspace or digital files to improve workflow.
Put a theoretical plan into physical motion.
Brainstorm with others to gather diverse solutions, then act on the best one.
Use your hands to fix or build something that requires precision.

birthday.title: The Architect of Reality
birthday.content:
To be born on 5 No'j is to possess a restless, brilliant mind that yearns to be used. You are a natural problem-solver who is not content with simply understanding how things work—you want to improve them. The number Five gifts you with a strong work ethic and a need for physical engagement, while No'j provides you with a vast reservoir of knowledge and creativity. You are the engineer, the strategist, the one who can turn a vague concept into a working prototype.

Your challenge may lie in an overactive mind that leads to anxiety or burnout. You might feel a constant pressure to be 'doing' and 'thinking' simultaneously. Your lesson is to learn to pace yourself, to allow the earthquake of your ideas to settle before rushing to build on the new ground. Your soul's purpose is to bridge the gap between the intellectual and the practical, showing the world that true wisdom is found in the application of knowledge.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for No'j (Brain/Idea) glowing with internal electric blue and white light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 5: a single glowing golden horizontal bar. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Flow of Harmony
number.content: The number Six represents the ultimate stability of flow. It is the energy of the six directions (East, West, North, South, Heart of Sky, Heart of Earth) coming into alignment. Six brings a gentle, rocking motion that stabilizes the efforts of the Five. It is associated with family, physical health, and the rhythm of life. This vibration does not force; it allows. It is the point in the cycle where the initial struggle of creation gives way to a sustainable momentum. Six asks us to attend to the details of our daily lives with grace and to find the balance between doing and being.

nawal.title: The Blade of Truth
nawal.content: Tijax is the double-edged obsidian knife, the healer and the warrior. It represents the power to cut away illness, negativity, and illusion to reveal the truth. Tijax is the surgeon who wounds in order to heal, and the warrior who fights to protect the sacred. It governs sudden shocks, decisive actions, and the release of suffering. This nawal is intense and sharp, clearing the path of obstacles. It reminds us that sometimes we must sever our attachment to what is comfortable in order to survive and thrive. It is the spark of flint that starts the fire.

combined_energy.title: Healing the Rhythm of Life
combined_energy.content: 6 Tijax brings a surprisingly gentle touch to the sharp energy of the Obsidian Blade. The harmonizing influence of Six softens the cut, making this a profound day for holistic healing. It is about finding the rhythm in your recovery or the balance in your truth-telling. Instead of a violent severance, today offers a surgical precision that aligns with the natural flow of your life. It is an excellent day to cut out unhealthy habits, toxic relationships, or negative thought patterns, but to do so with a sense of self-care rather than self-punishment. The energy supports practical adjustments to your routine that promote long-term well-being.
notes: Schedule a medical check-up, massage, or acupuncture session.
Clean out a closet or drawer, physically cutting ties with the old.
Have a difficult conversation with the intent of restoring harmony.
Identify one daily habit that drains you and surgically remove it.
Meditate on the 'Heart of Sky and Heart of Earth' to center yourself.

birthday.title: The Gentle Surgeon
birthday.content:
To be born on 6 Tijax is to be a healer of the highest order. You possess the incisive clarity of the obsidian knife, but it is tempered by the nurturing stability of the number Six. You are likely the person friends turn to when they need the 'hard truth' delivered with love. You have a talent for seeing exactly where the problem lies—whether in a body, a machine, or a relationship—and knowing precisely what needs to be adjusted to restore flow.

Your challenge is that you may be prone to taking on the suffering of others or becoming overly critical when things are out of alignment. You feel the dissonance of the world acutely. Your soul's path is to master the art of detachment—to wield the knife of truth without cutting yourself. You are here to show that healing is not a one-time event but a daily rhythm, a constant pruning that allows life to flourish.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Tijax (Obsidian/Knife) glowing with internal reflective silver and volcanic red light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 6: a glowing golden horizontal bar with one dot centered above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Peak of Reflection
number.content: The number Seven stands at the top of the pyramid, the midpoint of the thirteen-day cycle. It represents the point of maximum visibility, where we can look back at where we started and forward to where we are going. Seven is a mystical number, representing the perfect balance between the spiritual and material worlds. It is an energy of pause, reflection, and calibration. Unlike the active numbers, Seven asks us to stop and assess. It creates a space for choices to be weighed without pressure. It is the breath at the top of the climb before the descent begins.

nawal.title: The Storm of Compassion
nawal.content: Kawoq is the energy of the community, the turtle, and the gathering storm. It represents the collective family and the compassionate, sometimes overwhelming, force of the divine feminine. Kawoq is the midwife, bringing new life into the world through the intensity of labor. It governs abundance, fertility, and the cleansing power of rain. While it is nurturing, Kawoq can also be tempestuous, washing away what is not true to the heart of the community. It asks us to consider the welfare of the group over the individual and to prepare for the rebirth of our reality.

combined_energy.title: The Eye of the Storm
combined_energy.content: 7 Kawoq places us in the calm center of the swirling energies of life. The reflective nature of Seven pauses the intense, generative power of the Kawoq storm, allowing us to understand the deeper purpose of the changes we are experiencing. This is a day to look at your role within your family and community from a spiritual perspective. Are you being swept away by the collective drama, or are you anchoring the light? The energy supports a deep, compassionate review of your relationships and your contribution to the whole. It is a time to incubate your dreams in the safety of the shell, knowing that the storm outside is preparing the soil for your growth.
notes: Take a pause from social media to assess your own feelings vs. the collective's.
Reflect on a recent 'stormy' period in your life: what was born from it?
Gather with a small, trusted group to share deep feelings.
Visualize yourself as the eye of the storm—calm amidst chaos.
Assess the health of your 'community'—who supports you, who do you support?

birthday.title: The Compassionate Mystic
birthday.content:
To be born on 7 Kawoq is to be a lightning rod for collective emotion and spiritual insight. You stand at the crossroads of the human family, feeling the joys and sorrows of those around you with intense clarity. The number Seven gives you a mystical, reflective nature, while Kawoq provides you with a heart big enough to hold the storm. You are likely a natural counselor, teacher, or community organizer—someone who can see the spiritual lesson within the worldly struggle.

Your challenge is learning to manage the flood of empathy so you do not drown in it. You may struggle with boundaries, feeling responsible for healing everyone's pain. Your soul's lesson is to understand that you are the midwife, not the mother; you assist in the birthing process, but you cannot carry the labor for others. By maintaining your own spiritual balance (the Seven), you become a safe harbor for others in the storm.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Kawoq (Storm/Turtle) glowing with internal rain-blue and lightning-violet light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 7: a glowing golden horizontal bar with two dots centered above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Weaver of Justice
number.content: The number Eight represents the octave, the resumption of the cycle on a higher level. It is the number of harmony, justice, and pure energy. In the Mayan tradition, Eight is often associated with the 'male' or projective energy, balancing the reflective Seven. It represents the wholeness of the weaving, where the threads come together to form a pattern. Eight is the day of the art, the ceremony, and the return of energy. It asks us to organize the insights gained at the peak (Seven) and begin to integrate them back into the fabric of reality.

nawal.title: The Hero of Light
nawal.content: Ajpu is the sun, the blowgunner, the spiritual warrior, and the artist. It represents the divine spark within the human being—the 'light' of consciousness. Ajpu is the nawal of the hero's journey, the one who overcomes trials to claim their divinity. It governs hunting, precision, and the clarity of purpose. This energy is victorious, creative, and connected to the cycles of the sun. It asks us to stand in our own light, to act with nobility, and to recognize that we are co-creators of our reality. It is the flower of life blooming in full glory.

combined_energy.title: Ceremony of the Inner Sun
combined_energy.content: 8 Ajpu is one of the most auspicious days in the calendar. It represents the perfect harmony of spiritual light. The wholeness of Eight combines with the solar power of Ajpu to create a day of total alignment between the human and the divine. This is the 'Golden Day,' a time to honor the light within yourself and others. It is ideal for ceremonies, artistic performances, or any act that celebrates life. The energy is clear, bright, and regal. You are asked to wear your crown today, to walk with the dignity of a spiritual warrior who knows who they are. Shadows have nowhere to hide under this zenith sun.
notes: Perform a personal ceremony to honor your life's purpose.
Create art or music that expresses your inner light.
Stand up for a cause you believe in with noble confidence.
Spend time in direct sunlight to recharge your solar plexus.
Acknowledge the divinity in everyone you meet today (Namaste in action).

birthday.title: The Solar King/Queen
birthday.content:
To be born on 8 Ajpu is to carry the energy of a spiritual royalty. You are a beacon of light, possessing a natural charisma and a clear sense of purpose that draws others to you. The number Eight grants you a deep sense of harmony and justice, while Ajpu fills you with the courage of the hero. You are likely an artist, a performer, or a leader who shines brightest when you are authentic. Life for you is a quest, a series of trials that you face not with fear, but with the certainty of your own divine origin.

Your challenge is overcoming the ego. The brightness of your light can sometimes blind you to the needs of others, or you may fall into the trap of thinking you must be 'perfect' to be worthy. Your soul's journey is to learn that true heroism is about service, not glory. You are here to remind the world that everyone carries a spark of the divine, using your light to ignite the candles of those around you.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Ajpu (Sun/Hunter) glowing with internal solar gold and white light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 8: a glowing golden horizontal bar with three dots centered above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Cycle of Gestation
number.content: The number Nine represents the feminine, the mysterious, and the completion of cycles. It is the number of life, associated with the nine months of human gestation. Nine brings an energy of patience, depth, and the quiet work that happens in the dark before birth. It is often linked to the ancestors and the 'Great Mystery.' This vibration is not about rushing to the finish line, but about ensuring that the creation is fully formed and viable. It asks for trust in the timing of the universe and a willingness to sit in the unknown.

nawal.title: The Primordial Ocean
nawal.content: Imox is the spirit of the water, the collective unconscious, and the source of all things. It represents the dream world, intuition, and the chaotic, creative soup from which reality emerges. Imox is the lizard, the crocodile that floats half-submerged. It governs our emotions, our sanity, and our connection to the telepathic web of life. This nawal is fluid, irrational, and deeply psychic. It can bring immense creativity or emotional overwhelm. It asks us to embrace the flow, to trust our gut feelings, and to recognize that we are not separate drops, but part of a vast ocean.

combined_energy.title: Dreaming the World into Being
combined_energy.content: 9 Imox is a day of deep, submerged magic. The gestational energy of Nine combines with the watery depths of Imox to create a womb-like atmosphere. This is a day where the veil between the conscious and unconscious is incredibly thin. It is a time for dreaming, for artistic incubation, and for connecting with the feminine lineage. The energy is soft, nebulous, and highly psychic. You may feel more emotional or sensitive than usual; this is simply your antennae picking up the collective frequency. Do not try to force logic onto today's experiences. Instead, float in the mystery and let your intuition guide the ripening of your soul's desires.
notes: Pay close attention to your dreams; keep a journal by your bed.
Spend time near a body of water (ocean, lake, river, or bath).
Engage in free-flow writing or painting without a plan.
Avoid crowded places if you feel psychically porous.
Trust your 'crazy' ideas—they are seeds from the collective mind.

birthday.title: The Dreamer of the Deep
birthday.content:
To be born on 9 Imox is to be a bridge between the dream world and waking reality. You are a deeply sensitive and intuitive soul, often feeling the emotions of others as if they were your own. The number Nine gives you a connection to the mysteries of life and death, while Imox grants you access to the collective unconscious. You are likely an artist, a visionary, or a healer who works with the psyche. You understand that reality is fluid and that imagination is the precursor to matter.

Your challenge is staying grounded. The vastness of your internal ocean can sometimes lead to confusion, escapism, or difficulty distinguishing your feelings from the collective's. You may struggle with 'reality' in the conventional sense. Your soul's path is to learn to navigate these waters without drowning—to bring the pearls of wisdom from the deep up to the surface. You are here to remind the world of the power of the invisible.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Imox (Water/Dragon) glowing with internal aqua and deep teal light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 9: a glowing golden horizontal bar with four dots centered above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Hand of Manifestation
number.content: The number Ten represents the two hands coming together—cooperation and the full realization of the form. It is the number of manifestation, responsibility, and community agreement. Ten takes the gestational energy of Nine and births it into the world. It is a solid, even number that brings things to a head. It represents the laws of society and the agreements we make with each other. On this day, the focus is on the concrete results of our actions and the legacy we are building. It is a day to shake hands and seal the deal.

nawal.title: The Breath of Spirit
nawal.content: Iq' is the wind, the breath of life, and the voice of the spirit. It represents communication, inspiration, and the vital force that animates all living things. Iq' is the messenger that carries the seeds of change. It governs the mind, the lungs, and the power of words. This nawal can be gentle like a breeze or destructive like a hurricane. It cleanses, purifies, and shifts energy rapidly. It asks us to speak our truth, to breathe deeply, and to be willing to change our minds when the wind shifts direction.

combined_energy.title: Manifesting Through the Word
combined_energy.content: 10 Iq' brings the power of the spoken word into concrete reality. The manifesting energy of Ten combines with the communicative force of Iq' to create a day where 'abracadabra' (I create as I speak) is literally true. This is a powerful day for agreements, contracts, and public speaking. The wind is no longer just blowing aimlessly; it is being channeled into a structure (Ten) that can drive windmills or fill sails. It is a time to take responsibility for your communication—to ensure your words align with your actions. The energy supports clearing the air in relationships and establishing new, healthier agreements based on truth.
notes: Sign contracts or make verbal agreements with clear intent.
Practice conscious breathwork to ground your nervous system.
Speak a desire out loud to 'crystallize' it into the physical world.
Clear the air in a misunderstanding; the wind supports resolution.
Write a manifesto or mission statement for your life or project.

birthday.title: The Voice of Change
birthday.content:
To be born on 10 Iq' is to be a powerful communicator and a catalyst for social evolution. You have the gift of gab, but it is backed by the weight of responsibility (Ten). You do not just speak to hear your own voice; you speak to build, to connect, and to manifest. You are likely a writer, a speaker, or a negotiator—someone who understands how to use language to shape reality. The wind moves through you, bringing constant new ideas and a need for freedom.

Your challenge is consistency. The wind can be fickle, and you may find yourself changing direction often or struggling to stick to the agreements you've made. Your words have immense power, so you must learn to wield them with care, avoiding gossip or harshness. Your soul's purpose is to be a breath of fresh air in stagnant situations, using your voice to bring people together and to inspire concrete, positive change in your community.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Iq' (Wind/T-shape) glowing with internal white and pale blue light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 10: two glowing golden horizontal bars stacked vertically. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Force of Dissolution
number.content: The number Eleven represents dissonance, trial, and the breaking of patterns. It is often seen as a challenging number, not because it is 'bad,' but because it forces us to shed what is no longer working. Eleven is the energy of the seed husk cracking open so the sprout can emerge. It brings a lack of balance that compels movement. This vibration asks us to let go of our rigid structures and expand. It is the necessary chaos that precedes a higher order. Eleven days are often intense, requiring flexibility and non-attachment.

nawal.title: The Dawn of Revelation
nawal.content: Aq'ab'al is the dawn, the twilight, the threshold between night and day. It represents the moment when the light conquers the darkness, bringing clarity and new beginnings. Aq'ab'al is the energy of potential, of the 'idea' becoming the 'reality.' It governs duality, polarization, and the sudden realization of truth. This nawal is optimistic but realistic; it knows the dark exists but chooses the light. It asks us to wake up, to wipe the sleep from our eyes, and to seize the opportunities that are emerging on the horizon.

combined_energy.title: Breaking Through to the Light
combined_energy.content: 11 Aq'ab'al is a high-voltage day of breakthrough. The pattern-breaking energy of Eleven combines with the dawning light of Aq'ab'al to create a powerful moment of realization. This is a day where old structures may crumble, but the light that shines through the cracks is blindingly beautiful. You may experience a sudden shift in perspective, a 'waking up' from a long slumber. The energy can feel unstable, like the flickering light of a candle in the wind, but it is driving you toward a higher clarity. Don't cling to the night; let the dawn strip away your illusions. It is a time to reinvent yourself.
notes: Embrace a sudden change of plans as a divine redirection.
Wake up before sunrise to witness the physical dawn and set intentions.
Identify a belief system that feels 'tight' and consciously break out of it.
Look for the opportunity hidden within a challenge or conflict.
Allow yourself to feel the tension of the threshold without rushing to resolve it.

birthday.title: The Harbinger of Dawn
birthday.content:
To be born on 11 Aq'ab'al is to be a transformer, a person who lives on the edge of the new. You are comfortable in the liminal spaces where others feel anxious. The number Eleven gives you a restless, seeking spirit that is never satisfied with the status quo, while Aq'ab'al provides you with the optimism to see the potential for a new day in every ending. You are likely an innovator, a revolutionary, or a guide who helps others navigate transitions. Your life is a series of reinventions.

Your challenge is that your life can feel like a rollercoaster. The instability of the Eleven means you may struggle to find a resting place. You might be prone to breaking things—relationships, jobs, habits—just to feel the rush of the new. Your soul's path is to learn that the dawn is a daily occurrence, not a crisis. You are here to show others that change is not something to be feared, but the very mechanism by which the light enters.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Aq'ab'al (Dawn/Bat) glowing with internal orange-gold and shadow-black light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 11: two glowing golden horizontal bars with one dot centered above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Crystal of Understanding
number.content: The number Twelve represents the grouping together of all parts to form a coherent whole. It is the number of understanding, retrospection, and the 'village council.' Twelve takes the chaotic expansion of Eleven and integrates it into wisdom. It is a stable, complex vibration that looks back at the entire cycle and makes sense of the journey. This energy is communal and cooperative, recognizing that the sum is greater than its parts. It asks us to synthesize our experiences and to find the common thread that binds them.

nawal.title: The Net of Abundance
nawal.content: K'at is the net, the spiderweb, and the gathering bag. It represents the principle of connection—how we are all tied together in the web of life. K'at is the energy of abundance, gathering resources, and holding space. However, it also represents the trap; the net can catch food, or it can tangle us. This nawal governs memories, debts, and the baggage we carry. It asks us to sort through our net, keeping what nurtures us and releasing what weighs us down. It is an energy of organization, burning the chaff to reveal the grain.

combined_energy.title: Gathering the Wisdom of the Cycle
combined_energy.content: 12 K'at is a day of great accumulation and sorting. The integrating energy of Twelve combines with the gathering power of K'at to create a massive 'harvest' of experience. This is the time to look at everything you have collected—physically, emotionally, and spiritually—during this trecena. What is gold, and what is dross? The energy supports a comprehensive review of your life's 'net.' It is a day to bundle your wisdom, to pay off debts, and to untangle the knots in your relationships. You have the understanding now to see how everything is connected. Use this day to organize your abundance so it serves you rather than enslaves you.
notes: Review your finances or resources; organize your physical assets.
Release a mental burden or 'old story' that is cluttering your mind.
Gather with your community to share resources or wisdom.
untangle a complex problem by looking at how the parts relate to the whole.
Donate physical items you no longer need to clear space for the new.

birthday.title: The Keeper of the Web
birthday.content:
To be born on 12 K'at is to be a master connector and a storehouse of memory. You have a mind that naturally sorts and categorizes experiences, seeing the hidden threads that link people and events. The number Twelve gives you a deep desire for understanding and community, while K'at provides you with the ability to gather resources and people together. You are likely the 'glue' in your social circle, the archivist, or the one who holds space for everyone else. You carry an abundance of talent and potential.

Your challenge is hoarding—whether it is physical clutter, emotional baggage, or too many responsibilities. You may feel trapped by your own net, unable to move because you are carrying so much. Your soul's path is to learn the art of release. You must constantly curate your life, understanding that true abundance requires flow, not stagnation. You are here to teach the world that we are all connected, but that we must remain free within the web.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for K'at (Net/Lizard) glowing with internal harvest-gold and fibrous brown light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 12: two glowing golden horizontal bars with two dots centered above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Ascension of Spirit
number.content: The number Thirteen is the completion, the ascension, and the return to the source. It represents the highest vibration of the cycle, where the wave returns to the ocean. Thirteen is mystical, transformative, and powerful. It is the number of the ancestors and the spirit world. On this day, the energy is not about doing, but about becoming. It is a time of synthesis, where the lessons of the previous twelve days are fully integrated and transcended. Thirteen prepares us for the flight into the next trecena.

nawal.title: The Serpent of Wisdom
nawal.content: Kan is the feather serpent, the Kundalini energy, and the spark of life force in the blood. It represents power, evolution, and the transmutation of energy. Kan is the wisdom that comes from the body, the instinct, and the DNA. It governs sexuality, justice, and the truth that is felt rather than thought. This nawal is intense and demanding; it asks us to shed our old skins like the snake and to embody our power. It is the energy of the magician who knows how to move energy to create change.

combined_energy.title: The Transmutation of Power
combined_energy.content: 13 Kan brings the Aj trecena to a magnificent, high-frequency close. The ascending energy of Thirteen combines with the evolutionary power of the Serpent to create a day of profound transformation. This is the moment where the stable authority of Aj (Day 1) evolves into spiritual wisdom. The energy is electric, vital, and surging. You are asked to shed the skin of who you were thirteen days ago and step fully into your power. It is a day to honor the life force within you, to trust your instincts, and to channel your energy toward a higher purpose. The staff of authority has become the wand of the magician.
notes: Engage in physical movement (yoga, dance) to move stagnant energy.
Meditate on your spine as a channel of light connecting Earth and Sky.
Release an old identity or label that no longer fits your evolved self.
Trust your body's wisdom over your mind's logic today.
Offer gratitude for the vitality and health that sustains you.

birthday.title: The Master of Evolution
birthday.content:
To be born on 13 Kan is to be a powerhouse of spiritual and physical energy. You represent the culmination of the cycle, holding the wisdom of the ancients in your blood. The number Thirteen gives you a connection to the otherworld and a sense of completing things, while Kan grants you an intense vitality and charisma. You are a natural leader, healer, or teacher who transforms others simply by your presence. You have a magnetic quality that can be both seductive and intimidating.

Your challenge is managing the intensity of your own power. You may struggle with the 'shadow side' of Kan—manipulation, ego, or misuse of sexual energy. Because you feel everything so intensely, you must learn to ground yourself or risk burning out. Your soul's purpose is to master the serpent energy, using it not for personal gain but for the evolution of consciousness. You are here to demonstrate that true power is the ability to constantly shed your skin and be reborn closer to the light.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Kan (Serpent/Snake) glowing with internal electric red and emerald green light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 13: two glowing golden horizontal bars with three dots centered above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.
